surface_or_claim: `kolm serve --mcp` exposes local artifacts as MCP tools.
evidence: `cli/kolm.js` accepts only `--mcp` for serve and imports `services/mcp/server.js`; smoke `tools/list` found fixture artifacts.
code_truth: Local artifact MCP exists for stdio JSON-RPC and optional localhost HTTP JSON-RPC.
risk: Good base for agent integration if claims stay narrow.
recommended_action: Keep this as the launch core and add MCP contract tests.

surface_or_claim: MCP server supports standard discovery and invocation.
evidence: `services/mcp/server.js` implements `initialize`, `tools/list`, `tools/call`, and `ping`.
code_truth: Basic JSON-RPC methods exist, but no resources, prompts, subscriptions, or SDK-backed protocol layer are implemented.
risk: Agents can run artifacts, but broader MCP capability claims should not be implied.
recommended_action: Document the implemented method subset and add method-level tests.

surface_or_claim: MCP supports stdio or HTTP.
evidence: `startHttp` serves GET `/health` and POST `/mcp`, binding to `127.0.0.1`; `cmdServe` passes `--http` and `--port`.
code_truth: HTTP JSON-RPC is real and localhost-bound.
risk: Useful for local agents, but docs must not call it SSE.
recommended_action: Document HTTP as JSON-RPC POST and add smoke tests.

surface_or_claim: Project `kolm.yaml` can define artifact discovery and K-score floors.
evidence: `discoverSources` reads `findProjectConfig`, artifact globs, project name, and `k_min`; `src/project.js` resolves glob paths.
code_truth: Project-scoped tool names and artifact discovery exist.
risk: This is a useful governance hook if score scale is fixed.
recommended_action: Add project fixture tests for globs, prefixes, and gates.

surface_or_claim: MCP only exposes artifacts that load and verify.
evidence: `listTools` calls `inspectArtifact`; `loadArtifact` verifies manifest signature and throws on invalid signature.
code_truth: Badly signed artifacts are skipped during listing or fail during calls.
risk: Solid artifact integrity boundary for private artifacts.
recommended_action: Keep signature verification before metadata display and add corrupted-artifact MCP tests.

surface_or_claim: Harness install is previewable before writing.
evidence: `kolm install <harness>` prints a target and config snippet unless `--apply` is passed.
code_truth: Users can inspect generated config before filesystem writes.
risk: Good safety posture for local agent config changes.
recommended_action: Keep preview default and test all harness snippets.

surface_or_claim: CLI lifecycle hooks exist for compile, run, and bench.
evidence: `src/hooks.js` parses `kolm.yaml` hook events; `cli/kolm.js` dispatches Pre/Post compile, run, and bench events.
code_truth: Hook plumbing is implemented for CLI commands.
risk: Hooks can support governance if tested and documented as CLI-only.
recommended_action: Add hook parser and block/allow regression tests.

surface_or_claim: `kolm serve --mcp` supports SSE.
evidence: `public/serve.html` claims `mcp/0.1 + sse`; `services/mcp/server.js` implements stdio and HTTP POST `/mcp`, with no SSE route.
code_truth: SSE is not implemented in the artifact MCP server.
risk: Clients following the serve page can configure the wrong transport.
recommended_action: Remove SSE wording or implement a tested SSE transport.

surface_or_claim: Agents discover `kolm serve --mcp` without configuration.
evidence: `public/serve.html` says agents discover it without configuration; implementation requires MCP client config or `kolm install`.
code_truth: There is no `.well-known` writer or OS-level discovery mechanism.
risk: Buyers can expect zero-config onboarding that fails locally.
recommended_action: Change copy to explicit client config or build discovery files per harness.

surface_or_claim: Serve writes `.well-known/mcp.json` next to artifacts.
evidence: The serve page claims a `.well-known/mcp.json`; source search found no writer for that file in MCP serve paths.
code_truth: No well-known capability file is emitted.
risk: Docs can mislead MCP client setup and security review.
recommended_action: Remove claim or implement and test file emission.

surface_or_claim: MCP clients exchange a short-lived bearer token from the artifact manifest.
evidence: `public/serve.html` describes a short-lived bearer token exchange; `services/mcp/server.js` has no auth or token exchange for local HTTP.
code_truth: Local HTTP is unauthenticated localhost JSON-RPC.
risk: Network-agent claims and security posture are overstated.
recommended_action: Label HTTP localhost-only and design token auth before network exposure.

surface_or_claim: Serve binds `127.0.0.1:7327` by default.
evidence: The serve page shows `127.0.0.1:7327`; code default is `8765` for HTTP, while stdio has no port.
code_truth: Default port docs drift from implementation.
risk: Users and docs tests can chase the wrong endpoint.
recommended_action: Pick one port and generate docs from CLI constants.

surface_or_claim: `kolm serve --mcp support-triage.kolm` serves that specific artifact.
evidence: `public/serve.html` and client snippets include an artifact arg; `cmdServe` ignores positional args and always passes the artifact directory/project cwd.
code_truth: Single-artifact serve is not implemented.
risk: Agents may expose all local artifacts when docs imply one artifact.
recommended_action: Implement explicit artifact allowlist or remove positional examples.

surface_or_claim: MCP runtime patches network egress before recipes run.
evidence: Agentic-coding copy says the harness patches `fetch`, `http`, `https`, `net`, `tls`, and `dns`; `services/mcp/server.js` calls `runArtifact` directly, while egress patching lives in `benchmarkArtifact`.
code_truth: MCP calls rely on source scanning and vm sandboxing, not benchmark egress patching.
risk: Zero-runtime-egress claims can be overstated for MCP calls.
recommended_action: Move egress guard into `runArtifact` or narrow copy to benchmark evidence.

surface_or_claim: Every MCP answer carries a verified HMAC receipt chain.
evidence: `tools/call` returns `r.receipt`; `runArtifact` creates an unsigned `rs-1-run` metadata object, not an HMAC chain over the call.
code_truth: Artifact signatures verify on load, but per-call local receipts are not signed.
risk: Audit and procurement claims can overstate proof quality.
recommended_action: Implement signed local run receipts or relabel as run metadata.

surface_or_claim: `kolm logs` includes MCP `tools/call` rows.
evidence: CLI help says MCP calls append to `~/.kolm/logs/runs.jsonl`; source search shows append calls in CLI run/bench only, not MCP server.
code_truth: MCP calls are not appended to the local run log.
risk: Operators can miss agent activity despite docs saying it is logged.
recommended_action: Add `appendRunLog` support to MCP or narrow `kolm logs` wording.

surface_or_claim: MCP advertises and gates K-score on a 0..1 scale.
evidence: Public docs use gates like `>=0.85`; fixture MCP smoke returned K-score composites like `424.57`; `k_min` compares raw values directly.
code_truth: Serve-time K-score governance is scale-drifted.
risk: Low-quality artifacts can pass floors if scores are not normalized consistently.
recommended_action: Normalize K-score schema and rebuild fixtures before relying on `k_min`.

surface_or_claim: Docs agree on the Claude/Claude Code config target.
evidence: Live integrations mention `~/.claude/mcp.json`; docs mention `~/.claude/mcp.json`; agentic-coding mentions `~/.config/claude/mcp.json`; CLI writes `~/.claude/settings.json` for `claude-code`.
code_truth: Config paths are inconsistent across public copy and installer.
risk: Users can edit the wrong file and think MCP is broken.
recommended_action: Choose canonical Claude Desktop vs Claude Code targets and split docs accordingly.

surface_or_claim: `kolm doctor` verifies harness config and ports.
evidence: Quickstart says doctor verifies wiring; `cmdDoctor` checks config, API key, cloud reachability, secret, Node, docker, Anthropic key, project config, and artifact counts only.
code_truth: Doctor does not inspect MCP harness config or port listeners.
risk: Broken agent config can pass doctor.
recommended_action: Add harness config validation and MCP smoke checks to doctor.

surface_or_claim: `kolm install cline --apply` wires MCP like other harnesses.
evidence: Code writes `.clinerules/kolm.md` with instructions; it does not create an MCP config entry.
code_truth: Cline integration is an instructional rule, not MCP wiring.
risk: The install command can be misread as fully wired agent integration.
recommended_action: Label Cline as rule-only or implement client-specific MCP config.

surface_or_claim: `kolm install continue` safely merges Continue config.
evidence: Code appends an `mcpServers` YAML block unless `name: kolm` exists anywhere; it does not parse YAML or preserve schema semantics.
code_truth: Continue config merging is string-based.
risk: Existing configs can become malformed or duplicate blocks.
recommended_action: Use a YAML parser or write a clearly scoped project-local snippet.

surface_or_claim: `allowed-tools` governs what sub-agents may call.
evidence: `kolm.yaml` parses `allowed_tools` and MCP metadata carries `allowed_tools`; `tools/call` does not enforce nested tool permissions.
code_truth: Allowed-tool metadata is advisory only.
risk: Security reviewers may assume a real capability boundary.
recommended_action: Mark as metadata or enforce at runtime when sub-agent calls exist.

surface_or_claim: Generated sidecars tell agents the correct MCP tool name.
evidence: No-project sidecars emit `mcp__kolm__<artifact>`, while global MCP tools are named plain `<artifact>`; project tools use `mcp__<project>__<artifact>`.
code_truth: Tool naming can drift outside project mode.
risk: Agents can call a non-existent tool from the generated sidecar.
recommended_action: Align global tool names or sidecar fallback names and add a fixture test.

surface_or_claim: Claude/Cursor templates use existing root CLI commands.
evidence: Live templates mention `kolm query` and `kolm verify`; root CLI dispatch has no top-level `query` or `verify`.
code_truth: Templates can instruct agents to run failing commands.
risk: Agent onboarding can fail immediately.
recommended_action: Update templates to `kolm rag query`, `kolm inspect`, `kolm eval`, or add compatibility aliases.

surface_or_claim: Public agent templates share the current K-score policy.
evidence: Claude/Cursor templates say `>=0.70` ships; docs and project schema use `0.85`; fixture manifests show unbounded composites.
code_truth: Agent guidance conflicts with current gate language and score data.
risk: Agents can recommend shipping below the public gate.
recommended_action: Regenerate templates from one K-score policy source.

surface_or_claim: `sdk/mcp` matches the current artifact MCP story.
evidence: `sdk/mcp/server.mjs` exposes `recipe_*` cloud tools through the legacy scoped cloud SDK and `RECIPE_API_KEY`; live docs emphasize `kolm serve --mcp` artifact tools.
code_truth: There are two different MCP products with different brands, auth, and data paths.
risk: Developers can install the wrong MCP server for artifact-local workflows.
recommended_action: Separate legacy cloud MCP from artifact MCP or retire/update `sdk/mcp`.

surface_or_claim: The shipped compile action tracks the CLI contract.
evidence: Action runs `kolm compile ... --json`, parses `.artifact_path`, `.k_score`, `.id`, and calls `kolm verify`; root CLI has no top-level `verify` and cloud compile prints progress text around outputs.
code_truth: The action contract is likely broken without a compatibility shim.
risk: A shipped CI integration can fail for users on first run.
recommended_action: Add an action smoke test or update action to current CLI outputs and commands.

surface_or_claim: The VS Code extension is preview and source-backed.
evidence: Live integrations label VS Code preview and point to `sdk/vscode`; repository contains `sdk/vscode` files.
code_truth: Preview status matches the repo presence.
risk: Preview remains honest only if it is not labeled shipped before packaging and tests exist.
recommended_action: Keep preview label until packaged and tested.

surface_or_claim: MCP and install behavior are launch-blocking tested.
evidence: Existing tests do not cover MCP server methods, install snippets, project `k_min`, sidecar tool names, or MCP logging.
code_truth: No launch-blocking MCP or harness-install tests were found.
risk: Agent integration regressions can ship silently.
recommended_action: Add MCP stdio/HTTP, install, sidecar, doctor, and log tests.

surface_or_claim: Public MCP docs are generated from implementation constants.
evidence: Serve/docs/integration pages disagree on ports, Claude config paths, transports, commands, receipt shape, and discovery behavior.
code_truth: MCP docs are manual and drift-prone.
risk: Users get inconsistent setup instructions.
recommended_action: Generate MCP docs from CLI constants and fixture smoke outputs.
sources: public/serve.html; public/docs.html; public/integrations.html; public/use-cases/agentic-coding.html; cli/kolm.js; services/mcp/server.js
